stories.title: Ticket dashboard: local static page rendering prd.json stories
stories.description: Build a zero-build local dashboard so a human can view the ticket backlog and live story state in a browser instead of a Notion board. Static HTML + vanilla JS/CSS only, living in a new dashboard/ directory. The page fetches ../prd.json and renders one card per story, grouped into columns by status. It is served with a plain static file server from the repo root (browsers block fetch over file://), e.g. `uv run python -m http.server 8000`, then opened at http://localhost:8000/dashboard/. No frameworks, no build step, no new Python dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: dashboard/index.html exists and contains all markup, styles, and script (or links only to sibling files inside dashboard/); no framework, bundler, or CDN dependency
When served from the repo root with a static file server, the page fetches prd.json and renders one card per story showing id, type, title, priority, and acceptance criteria
Cards are grouped into four columns by status: open, in_progress, blocked, done; a story with passes=true is visually marked as passing
If prd.json cannot be fetched or parsed, the page shows a readable error message instead of a blank screen
A comment at the top of dashboard/index.html documents the serve command and URL
A pytest test (tests/test_dashboard.py) asserts dashboard/index.html exists and references prd.json, and the full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Add 'you are headless' rule to all stage command prompts
stories.description: In test runs 3-4, stage agents that hit contradictory constraints ended their turn by asking the nonexistent human a question ('Two ways forward, your call') instead of reporting blocked in the status block; run 3's planner lost its entire plan text that way because only the final message survives a stage. Add an explicit rule to every stage command file (commands/PLAN.md, IMPLEMENT.md, TEST.md, REVIEW.md, and PRIME.md if it gives behavioral instructions): you are running headless, nobody will ever answer a question, and any contradiction or blocker must be reported as outcome=blocked in the structured status block with the reason in failure_reason. Evidence: observability/test_run1.md follow-up 2.
acceptance_criteria: Each of commands/PLAN.md, commands/IMPLEMENT.md, commands/TEST.md, commands/REVIEW.md contains an explicit headless rule: no human will answer questions, and contradictions/blockers must be reported as outcome=blocked in the status block instead of asking
The rule names the status block as the only channel for blockers (failure_reason carries the why)
A pytest test asserts the headless rule text is present in all four stage command files so it cannot silently regress
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Circuit breaker: trip immediately on repeated dead-on-arrival stages
stories.description: Test run 2 burned all iterations re-trying identical instant failures: the CLI exited non-zero with 0 tokens used and empty output, three times in a row, and the breaker never fired because the same_error_loops threshold (5) can never be reached before max_iterations (5) under default budgets. Add an instant-failure rule to adw/safety.py CircuitBreaker: a stage result with exit_code != 0 AND tokens_used == 0 AND empty raw output counts as dead-on-arrival; after 2 consecutive such results, open the circuit regardless of other thresholds. Make the threshold a budgets.json knob (instant_failure_cap, default 2) wired through SafetyConfig.from_budgets like permission_denial_cap. Evidence: observability/test_run1.md follow-up 1 and the run 2 row of its table.
acceptance_criteria: CircuitBreaker.record opens the circuit (returns a halt reason naming instant failure) after 2 consecutive dead-on-arrival results: exit_code != 0, tokens_used == 0, empty raw output
A successful or token-consuming stage result resets the consecutive counter
The threshold is read from configs/budgets.json key instant_failure_cap (default 2) via SafetyConfig.from_budgets
Unit tests cover: trip at 2 consecutive instant failures, reset on a live result, and config override of the cap
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Bug + trivial workflows so bug/chore tickets become runnable
stories.description: architecture.md names bug_plan_implement_test.py and trivial_implement_test.py but only feat_full_cycle.py exists, so ticket types bug and chore are schema-valid but unrunnable (audit finding A3, plans/improvements.md). Compose the two workflows from existing adw/ pieces: bug runs plan -> implement -> test (no review stage; the test stage's regression evidence is the gate), trivial runs implement -> test for chore-sized changes. Factor shared setup (branch, prompt composition, invoke wiring) out of feat_full_cycle.py rather than copying it.
acceptance_criteria: workflows/bug_plan_implement_test.py runs plan, implement, test stages for a ticket and honors the same breaker, budgets, and state bookkeeping as feat_full_cycle
workflows/trivial_implement_test.py runs implement, test stages with the same guarantees
Shared orchestration setup is factored so the three workflows do not triplicate prompt-composition or git bookkeeping code
Stage order remains owned by the workflow scripts, never by prompts
Unit tests cover stage sequencing of both new workflows with a fake invoke_fn
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Backlog runner: outer loop over open stories
stories.description: v1 runs one ticket per human invocation. Add workflows/run_backlog.py (improvements C6, snarktank/ralph pattern): a thin outer loop that, while open stories with passes=false remain, invokes the per-ticket workflow for the next story, honoring the circuit-breaker cooldown gate between tickets and stopping on the first blocked/halted outcome instead of skipping it. The inner safety model is untouched; the runner adds no new authority.
acceptance_criteria: workflows/run_backlog.py loops: pick next open story, run its workflow, repeat until no open stories remain
An active cooldown in state.json pauses or stops the loop; it is never silently overridden
A blocked or halted ticket stops the loop with a clear printed reason (no skipping to the next story)
A --max-tickets N flag bounds the loop (bounded everything, architecture.md principle 5)
Unit tests cover loop termination on: backlog empty, blocked outcome, cooldown, and max-tickets
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: GitHub Issues intake + done/blocked notifications (phone-facing backlog)
stories.description: Bridge to the remote-operation goal: tickets arranged from a phone, outcomes reported back to it. Intake: a sync script pulls GitHub Issues labeled adw from the repo into prd.json stories (issue body carries description + acceptance criteria; labels carry type/priority), so the GitHub mobile app becomes the write-capable taskboard. Outbound: when a workflow finishes a ticket, it opens/updates a PR for the adw/<id> branch and comments the outcome (done/blocked + reason) on the source issue, so GitHub push notifications reach the phone. Requires the existing git-credential token; no new services.
acceptance_criteria: workflows/sync_issues.py converts open GitHub Issues labeled adw into valid prd.json stories with a deterministic id mapping and never duplicates existing stories
Malformed issues are skipped with a printed reason, never written into prd.json
On ticket completion the workflow opens (or updates) a PR for adw/<id> and comments done/blocked + reason on the source issue
All GitHub calls go through the REST API with the locally stored credential; the scripts degrade with a clear error when offline
Unit tests cover the issue-to-story mapping and skip logic with fixture payloads (no live API in tests)
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Container isolation for unattended runs
stories.description: The loop currently runs on the bare Win11 host - the one open violation of the safety floor (improvements C1, highest-leverage safety item; adopt the textcortex/claude-code-sandbox Docker pattern). Run ADW_TICKET_RUN stage sessions inside a container with the repo mounted, no real credentials beyond a scoped git token, and network restricted to what stages need. This is the precondition for genuinely unattended operation and for any future --skip-permissions mode.
acceptance_criteria: Stage invocations run inside a container when an isolation flag is set; the host filesystem outside the repo mount is unreachable
No host credentials are visible inside the container beyond the scoped git push token
Hooks (PreToolUse guard, Stop checklist, autocommit) still function inside the container
A documented fallback runs stages on the host exactly as today when isolation is off
README documents the isolation setup and its limits
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Document stage: per-ticket change doc for the merge-gate human
stories.description: Add a fifth, post-gate 'document' stage so the merge-gate human reviews an organized technical delta (endpoints, schemas, components, pages, CLI surfaces) instead of raw diff + run logs. It runs exactly once per ticket, after the review stage passes the dual gate (outcome=success AND exit_signal=true) and before the workflow reports done - never inside the bounded plan->implement->test->review loop, and it can never send the ticket back to plan. The stage reads the ticket, git diff main...HEAD, and the run's prior stage outputs, then writes and commits docs/changes/<ticket-id>.md (kept forever; one file per ticket; docs/ is deliberately not a harness directory). Registration: add 'document' to STAGES in adw/state.py (adw/status.py validates stage names against it) and to STAGE_TOOLS in adw/invoke.py with scope Read, Glob, Grep, Write, the existing _GIT_READONLY rules, plus Bash(git add:*) and Bash(git commit:*); add "document": "sonnet" to configs/models.json. Prompt assets: commands/DOCUMENT.md (entry prompt; must state that the agent runs headless - nobody will answer questions, blockers go in the status block - and that the doc must be committed before stopping, since the Stop checklist's clean-tree rule applies to this writing stage) and stage_specs/document_feat.md (the contract). Doc template sections, each included only when that change-kind exists in the diff: What shipped; Surface changes with subsections Endpoints/APIs, Schemas & data, Components/pages, CLI/workflows; Behavior & breaking changes; How it was verified (acceptance criteria mapped to test evidence); Review notes; File map (changed files, one line of why each). Contract rules: every claim anchored in the actual diff (never the plan's intentions), organized delta not a diff dump, bounded length, no future-work speculation, README/architecture drift is noted in the doc as a suggested follow-up and never edited. Orchestrator: in adw/orchestrator.py run_ticket, after the dual gate passes, invoke the document stage once with exactly one retry on failure/timeout; persistent failure still returns done with a warning recorded on the TicketOutcome, and a breaker halt reason from the document stage downgrades to a warning rather than converting done into halted. No hook changes are needed.
acceptance_criteria: 'document' is registered in STAGES (adw/state.py) and STAGE_TOOLS (adw/invoke.py) with exactly Read, Glob, Grep, Write, the read-only git rules, Bash(git add:*), Bash(git commit:*); configs/models.json maps document to sonnet

commands/DOCUMENT.md and stage_specs/document_feat.md exist with the repo's standard front-matter (name, description, read_when, sdlc_stage), define the conditional section template (What shipped; Endpoints/APIs; Schemas & data; Components/pages; CLI/workflows; Behavior & breaking changes; How it was verified; Review notes; File map) and the anchored-in-diff / no-diff-dump / bounded-length / no-speculation rules; DOCUMENT.md states the headless rule and the commit-before-stop requirement
adw/orchestrator.py run_ticket invokes the document stage exactly once, only after review reports outcome=success with exit_signal=true, and never on iterations that loop back to plan

Document-stage failure or timeout is retried exactly once; persistent failure still returns outcome 'done' with the warning recorded on TicketOutcome; a breaker halt reason raised during the document stage becomes a warning, never a halted outcome

Unit tests with a fake invoke_fn cover: document runs once after the dual gate; document is not invoked when review fails or loops; document failure plus one retry still yields done with the warning recorded
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: CI gate: GitHub Actions pytest on every PR + branch protection on main
stories.description: Add an independent, agent-proof correctness gate that lives OUTSIDE the harness's trust boundary. Today the only test signal is the in-loop test stage's self-report plus the dual exit gate - all produced by the same stage agents that wrote the code, so a subtly-wrong agent can write a trivially-passing test, skip a failing one, or self-report green. Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs `uv run pytest -q` on every pull request targeting main, so the same suite runs where no stage agent can edit, skip, or misreport it. This is the precondition that makes any future auto-merge defensible (S-010 / improvements C3 is the in-harness mirror; this is the out-of-harness one). NOTE: the file goes in .github/workflows/ci.yml - this is GitHub's CI directory and is distinct from the repo-root workflows/ directory that holds the harness orchestrators; do not put CI yaml in workflows/. Branch protection itself (requiring the check before merge to main) is a one-time human repo-admin action - agents cannot change repo settings or push to main - so the agent-runnable scope is the workflow file plus README docs; enabling branch protection is a documented manual human step, explicitly out of agent scope and never a blocker to report.
acceptance_criteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml triggers on pull_request targeting main, sets up Python 3.11 and uv, runs `uv sync` then `uv run pytest -q`, and fails the check on any test failure; it uses no secrets and no network beyond dependency install (the suite is offline per README)
The CI yaml lives in .github/workflows/ (GitHub's directory), never in the repo-root workflows/ directory that holds harness orchestrators

README documents the one-time manual branch-protection step (require the CI check to pass before merging to main) with exact gh api or UI instructions, and states this is the agent-proof gate that makes auto-merge defensible

The ticket's automatable scope is limited to the workflow file and README docs; branch-protection enablement is labeled a manual human/admin action and is explicitly out of agent scope, so a headless run neither attempts it nor reports blocked on it
When a PR is opened for the work branch, the CI workflow runs and passes (the suite is green under Actions, verified at the merge gate, not in-stage)
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Deterministic test-evidence re-run in the orchestrator before accepting done
stories.description: From improvements C3 (frankbria/ralph-claude-code pattern), promoted out of task.md ideas. Today adw/orchestrator.py run_ticket accepts a ticket as done on the dual gate alone (review outcome=success AND exit_signal=true), which trusts the test stage's self-reported green suite. Add a deterministic re-run of `uv run pytest -q` inside run_ticket itself - the orchestrator's own subprocess, not a stage agent's self-report - after the dual gate passes and BEFORE the document stage runs. A non-zero result prevents done: the outcome downgrades to blocked/halted with the failing summary captured in state.json last_failure, and the document stage does not run. A green result lets the existing done path (document stage, then done) proceed unchanged. This is the in-harness mirror of S-009's CI gate and the prerequisite for auto-merging trivial tickets without a human reading the diff.
acceptance_criteria: run_ticket, after the dual gate passes and before invoking the document stage, runs `uv run pytest -q` via subprocess and inspects the exit code - the orchestrator's own check, never a stage self-report
A non-zero pytest result prevents done: outcome becomes blocked (or halted), the captured failing summary is written to state.json last_failure, and the document stage is not invoked
A green pytest result lets the existing document-then-done path proceed unchanged
The pytest command and its timeout are configs/budgets.json knobs, not hardcoded
Unit tests with a stubbed subprocess runner cover: green re-run still yields done, red re-run blocks done with the failure recorded; no real pytest is spawned in the unit test
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Repo-agnostic engine: run the harness against any target repo
stories.description: Every module hardcodes REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).parent.parent, so the harness only operates on its own repo. Make the engine resolve a target repo from ADW_REPO (else the git top-level of cwd, else the engine repo for self-hosting). Per-project mutable state (prd.json, state.json, run logs, .claude/settings.json) lives in the target repo; prompt/command assets and configs/ default to the engine but a target-side .adw/ override wins if present. Package the engine so it is installable and invokable from any repo. This is the unlock for using the harness to build other projects.
acceptance_criteria: A single resolver (e.g. adw/paths.py) returns the target root from ADW_REPO, else `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`, else the engine repo; all modules use it instead of the hardcoded REPO_ROOT
prd.json, state.json, and run logs are read/written in the target repo; commands/ and configs/ resolve from the engine unless a target .adw/commands or .adw/configs exists, which takes precedence
Running ADW_REPO=/path/to/project against a workflow operates entirely on that project (branch, commits, logs land there) and leaves the engine repo untouched
With ADW_REPO unset and cwd in the engine repo, behavior is unchanged (self-hosting preserved)
A documented .adw/ skeleton (prd.json, configs, .claude/settings.json) turns a fresh repo into a target with no code copied
Unit tests cover path resolution (env override, git-toplevel fallback, per-project config override) against a temp repo; full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: One-shot intake to backlog runner (manual; no daemon or triggers)
stories.description: Honoring the no-triggers decision, the harness adds no scheduler. Add workflows/poll_once.py: one pass that runs sync_issues (ingest adw-labeled GitHub issues into prd.json), then run_backlog over the open stories with the existing cooldown and breaker gates and a --max-tickets bound. One launch equals one pass: no loop, no timer, no background thread. README documents how a human can optionally wire it to Windows Task Scheduler or cron, explicitly outside the harness authority.
acceptance_criteria: workflows/poll_once.py runs sync_issues then run_backlog in one process; a sync failure (e.g. offline) stops before the backlog with a clear message and never runs on a partial sync
It adds no daemon, timer, or background thread: exactly one pass per invocation
--max-tickets and the cooldown gate are honored; an active cooldown stops the pass without overriding it
README documents optional OS-scheduler wiring as a human opt-in, clearly out of harness scope
Unit tests cover sync-then-backlog ordering, stop-on-sync-failure, and the max-tickets bound with injected fakes (no live API)
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: decompose stage: terse/phone tickets auto-expanded into acceptance criteria
stories.description: Phone-filed issues are painful to write with full acceptance criteria (issue #16 came in with quote-bar formatting and was skipped). Wire the currently-vestigial decompose model entry into a real read-only stage that, for a ticket lacking acceptance criteria, expands its title and body into structured criteria before the plan stage. Relax sync_issues so a terse issue (no Acceptance Criteria heading, leading quote bars or indentation tolerated) is accepted with empty criteria instead of skipped; the workflow runs decompose first and the orchestrator persists the proposed criteria to prd.json (agents never self-write prd).
acceptance_criteria: commands/DECOMPOSE.md plus a stage_specs contract and STAGE_TOOLS[decompose] (read-only: Read, Glob, Grep, and the read-only git rules) are registered; configs/models.json already maps decompose
A story with empty acceptance_criteria triggers the decompose stage before plan; the orchestrator writes the proposed criteria into the story in prd.json
A story that already has acceptance_criteria skips decompose entirely (no behavior change for fully-specified tickets)

sync_issues accepts an issue with no Acceptance Criteria section and tolerates leading quote bars/indentation, instead of skipping; a genuinely empty issue (no title and no body) is still skipped with a printed reason
Decompose obeys the headless rule: too-vague input is reported as outcome=blocked, never asked; its output is bounded
Unit tests cover: decompose runs only when criteria absent, criteria persisted to prd, sync accepts a terse/quote-barred issue; full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Per-stage progress comments on the source GitHub issue
stories.description: S-006 comments on the source issue only once, at the terminal outcome (done/blocked) when it opens the PR. For the phone-facing taskboard the human wants to watch progress live. Add per-stage progress comments: as a GH-sourced ticket advances stages, post a concise comment on the issue so the phone gets a running log. Best-effort (never affects outcome), bounded (one comment per stage transition, not per iteration), and only for GH-sourced stories (S-NNN have no source issue). Implemented by injecting a progress callback into adw/orchestrator.py run_ticket, like verify_fn, so the orchestrator remains the only component that posts to GitHub; stage agents never call GitHub directly.
acceptance_criteria: For a GH-<n> story, the orchestrator posts a progress comment on each stage transition (stage started/finished with outcome); S-NNN stories post nothing
Comments are best-effort: any GitHub, credential, or offline failure is printed and swallowed, never changing the outcome or halting the run
Comment volume is bounded: at most one comment per stage transition (no per-iteration spam)
The S-006 terminal done/blocked comment and PR are preserved, not duplicated
The progress poster is injected into run_ticket; agents never call GitHub directly and the orchestrator stays the sole authority
Unit tests with a fake GitHub client cover: per-stage comment for a GH story, none for S-NNN, failures swallowed; full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Dashboard: show ticket count badge on each status col
stories.description:
Body:
The dashboard renders columns (open / in_progress / blocked / done) but
doesn't show how many tickets are in each. Add a small count badge to each
column header so the backlog size is visible at a glance.
acceptance_criteria: Each column header shows a numeric badge with the count of cards in it
The badge updates correctly when prd.json has zero cards in a column (shows 0)
tests/test_dashboard.py still passes and the full suite stays green

stories.title: Quota-limit interruptions become resumable (quotad status), not blocked
stories.description: On a Claude subscription the 5-hour usage limit can halt a run mid-ticket. Today that is mishandled three ways. (A) When the breaker detects a usage limit (adw/safety.py detect_usage_limit) it opens the circuit with the generic circuit_cooldown_minutes (30 min), far shorter than the ~5h reset, so the scheduled poll retries every cooldown window and hits the wall again. (B) The usage halt returns outcome=halted, and adw/workflow_runner.py _finalize_story marks any non-done outcome status='blocked' — which pick_next_story never auto-picks again (tickets.py) — so a perfectly good ticket interrupted only by quota is sidelined until a human reopens it, and during one outage the breaker marches through and wrongly blocks several legitimate tickets one per cooldown cycle. (C) _finalize_story runs the whole-repo opus observer on every non-done outcome, so a usage halt spends a big agent call precisely when we just ran out of usage. Fix all three by making a quota interruption a first-class, auto-resumable state called 'quotad' (distinct from 'blocked' = needs human attention). The cooldown for a usage-limit halt is set to the reset time parsed from the failure text when present, else a configurable usage_limit_cooldown_minutes (default 300) from configs/budgets.json. The breaker/orchestrator surface a distinct 'quotad' outcome for the usage-limit halt only; other halts stay 'halted'. _finalize_story handles 'quotad' specially: status='quotad', a 'quotad' GitHub label, NO PR opened (work is incomplete), and the observer is skipped. pick_next_story treats 'quotad' stories as eligible so they resume automatically once the cooldown elapses (check_cooldown still gates when), while 'blocked' stays human-gated.
acceptance_criteria: 'quotad' is added to STORY_STATUSES in adw/tickets.py and accepted by load_prd validation and mark_story; existing statuses are unchanged

adw/safety.py: on a detected usage limit the breaker sets state.cooldown_until from a reset time parsed out of the failure text when one is present (e.g. an ISO timestamp or a 'resets'/'try again' clause), else from a configurable usage_limit_cooldown_minutes (default 300) read via SafetyConfig.from_budgets / configs/budgets.json; the generic circuit_cooldown_minutes no longer governs a usage-limit halt
The usage-limit halt is surfaced as a distinct 'quotad' outcome from adw/orchestrator.py run_ticket (not 'halted'); every other breaker halt still returns 'halted'

adw/workflow_runner.py _finalize_story handles a 'quotad' outcome by marking the story status='quotad', adding a 'quotad' issue label (best-effort, no-op for plain S-NNN), opening NO PR, and NOT running the observer; the existing done/blocked/halted handling is otherwise unchanged

adw/tickets.py pick_next_story (and pick_next_stories) treat status=='quotad' stories as eligible to pick alongside 'open', so a quota-interrupted ticket auto-resumes once its cooldown elapses; status=='blocked' remains excluded (human-gated)

Unit tests cover: reset-time parsed from a sample usage-limit message and the configurable fallback when absent; run_ticket returns 'quotad' for a usage-limit halt and 'halted' for a non-usage halt; _finalize_story on 'quotad' sets the status/label and skips both the PR and the observer; pick_next_story picks a 'quotad' story
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Phone-facing lifecycle board via GitHub stage labels (no new frontend)
stories.description: The only phone-facing board today is GitHub itself, and the orchestrator already maintains issue labels (adw/workflow_runner.py _set_run_label: in-progress/done/blocked, plus the observer's self-heal-suggested/needs-clarification). Make the issue reflect the SDLC stage the ticket is currently in, so a GitHub Projects board (a one-time human setup) can show Plan/Do/Check/Review-gate/Blocked columns without any new frontend, API, or backend. As a GH-sourced ticket advances stages, the orchestrator updates a mutually-exclusive stage label on the source issue (stage:plan, stage:implement, stage:test, stage:review, stage:document) via the same best-effort, injected per-stage-transition mechanism as the S-014 progress callback, so the orchestrator stays the only component that talks to GitHub and stage agents never do. Label changes are quiet (GitHub does not push phone notifications for label edits, unlike comments), so this adds board state without notification spam. Best-effort throughout: any GitHub/credential/offline failure is printed and swallowed and never changes the ticket outcome. Plain S-NNN stories (no source issue) are no-ops. The terminal in-progress/done/blocked/quotad labels and the S-014 per-stage comments are preserved.
acceptance_criteria: As a GH-<n> story advances stages, the orchestrator sets a mutually-exclusive stage label on the source issue (one of stage:plan, stage:implement, stage:test, stage:review, stage:document) reflecting the current stage; the previous stage label is removed when the next is added so exactly one stage label is present

Stage labels are driven from the orchestrator on each stage transition through an injected, best-effort callback (the same injection pattern as the S-014 progress_fn), so stage agents never call GitHub directly and the orchestrator remains the sole GitHub authority
All stage-label updates are best-effort: any GitHub, credential, or offline failure is printed and swallowed, never changing the ticket's outcome or halting the run
Plain S-NNN stories (no source issue) trigger no stage-label calls; the existing in-progress/done/blocked/quotad labels and the S-014 per-stage comments are preserved, not removed or duplicated

README documents the one-time manual GitHub Projects setup that maps these labels to board columns (Plan / Do / Check / Review-gate / Blocked), labeled an out-of-agent-scope human action that a headless run never attempts and never reports blocked on

Unit tests with a fake GitHub client cover: the stage label is set and the prior one swapped on each transition for a GH story, no stage-label calls for an S-NNN story, and a GitHub failure is swallowed
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Mid-ticket checkpoint: resume a quotad ticket from its last stage via state.json
stories.description: Today adw/orchestrator.py run_ticket always calls new_state(story.id) at entry, resetting iteration to 1 and the stage to plan, so a ticket interrupted by quota (S-015 'quotad') re-runs from plan on resume even though git already holds the prior stages' commits on the adw/<id> branch. That is correct (git-as-memory means the new plan stage sees the earlier work) but wastes tokens — the exact constraint that matters on a subscription. Add a bounded checkpoint/resume: when run_ticket starts a ticket that has an existing state.json for the SAME ticket_id left at a mid-pipeline stage (i.e. the prior run was interrupted, not finished), resume from that stage instead of starting fresh, re-using the persisted iteration count; completed stages whose outputs are already committed are not re-run. A fresh ticket (no prior state, or a state.json for a different ticket_id, or a finished run) behaves exactly as today. The resume must stay fully bounded: it still honors max_iterations and the circuit breaker, and a resumed run can never loop unbounded. This is leverage on our existing state.json hand-off, not a new memory layer.
acceptance_criteria: run_ticket resumes from an existing state.json when it is for the same story.id and was left at a mid-pipeline stage (a quota interruption), re-entering at that stage and preserving the persisted iteration count instead of calling new_state

A fresh ticket — no state.json, a state.json for a different ticket_id, or one whose run already finished — behaves exactly as today (new_state, iteration 1, stage plan); existing single-ticket behavior is unchanged
On resume, stages already completed in the prior run (their outputs committed to the work branch) are not re-invoked; the loop re-enters at the first incomplete stage
A resumed run stays bounded: it honors max_iterations and the circuit breaker exactly as a fresh run, and cannot loop unbounded

Unit tests with a fake invoke_fn cover: resume from a mid-pipeline state.json re-enters at the recorded stage with the iteration preserved; a fresh ticket and a different-ticket state.json both start fresh; a resumed run still halts at max_iterations
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Recognize and parse the real Claude subscription session-limit message
stories.description: The quotad work (S-015) was built against a guessed usage-limit format and never fired on the real subscription message captured 2026-06-20: "You've hit your session limit · resets 1:10am (America/Toronto)". detect_usage_limit() missed it (no 'session limit' pattern) and _parse_usage_reset() returned None (the reset clause is neither a Unix epoch nor ISO-8601), so a real session limit was misclassified as an output-decline halt (S-017). Add a 'session limit' detection pattern and a 'resets <h>[:<mm>] <am|pm> (<IANA tz>)' reset-clause parser that resolves the next future UTC instant via zoneinfo, with tzdata declared so it works on Windows and a try/except fallback so a missing/unknown tz degrades to the configured cooldown rather than raising. Hand-implemented (not dogfooded) per the quota_resilience_plan tzdata risk note.
acceptance_criteria: detect_usage_limit recognizes the real session-limit message; the existing four patterns still match their samples; benign prose still returns False

_parse_usage_reset returns the correct future UTC instant for 'resets 1:10am (America/Toronto)' given a frozen now, rolls to the next day when that time already passed, and returns None (never raises) for an unknown tz or an unparseable clause
A real session-limit stage result routes through the quotad path (USAGE_LIMIT_HALT_REASON), not output-decline or dead-on-arrival
tzdata is a declared dependency so timezone resolution works on Windows; if absent, reset parsing degrades to None rather than raising
Unit tests cover detection, reset parse (frozen now, same-day roll, unknown-tz None), and the quotad routing; full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Observer must not wipe the run's circuit-breaker cooldown
stories.description: adw/orchestrator.py run_observer wrote a fresh State (cooldown_until=None) to the run's state.json, overwriting the cooldown the breaker had just set on a halt (evidence: S-017's state.json showed cooldown_until=null despite the circuit having opened). That lets an auto-retry start before the cooldown elapses, defeating the breaker's pause for every non-quota halt (the quotad path already skips the observer). Fix: run_observer loads the existing state.json and carries cooldown_until forward when it belongs to the same ticket, preserving the pause while still threading last_failure into the observe prompt.
acceptance_criteria: After a halt that set cooldown_until, running the observer leaves cooldown_until intact in state.json while still presenting the failure reason to the observe stage
check_cooldown still reports an active cooldown after the observer has run on a non-quota halt
A leftover state.json for a different ticket does not leak its cooldown onto the observe run
Unit tests cover: cooldown survives an observer run, observer still receives last_failure, and a different-ticket cooldown is ignored; full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Scheduled poll pass is self-auditable via an out-of-repo log
stories.description: The registered \ADW\ task ran poll_once.py with no output redirection, so a 29-minute pass that committed nothing and exited 0 was unexplainable after the fact. Make poll_once self-log independent of how the task is registered: append one bounded, timestamped summary line per pass (start time, elapsed seconds, sync outcome, tickets run, stop reason) to a log outside the repo (default %LOCALAPPDATA%/adw/poll.log, overridable by --log-path / $ADW_POLL_LOG). A failure to write the log is swallowed and never affects the pass; the working tree is never touched. README documents the self-log and labels re-registering the scheduled task (for a redirected stdout) an out-of-agent-scope human/admin action.
acceptance_criteria: poll_once appends a single bounded timestamped summary line per invocation to a configurable log outside the repo, covering sync outcome, tickets run, and stop reason; a write failure is swallowed and never affects the pass
The log path is configurable (--log-path / $ADW_POLL_LOG) and the working tree is never touched
README documents the self-log and the one-time admin re-registration of the scheduled task, labeled an out-of-agent-scope human action
Unit tests cover the summary line for a synced/ran pass and a stop-before-backlog pass with the log path injected; full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Interrupted run strands a ticket in in_progress with no auto-recovery
stories.description:
## Problem

An interrupted run strands a ticket in `in_progress` forever, with no recovery.

Observed 2026-06-20: the hourly scheduled poll fired (`:34`), picked GH-46,
marked it `in_progress`, created `adw/GH-46`, and launched the plan stage. The
run was then interrupted (it raced an interactive session working the same
tree): 0-byte `iter01_plan_output.md`, no completion line in `poll.log`, and
`state.json` left at `stage=plan`. The ticket is now stuck:

- `pick_next_story` only returns `status in ("open", "quotad")`
  (`adw/tickets.py:147,168`), so an `in_progress` ticket is **never re-picked** —
  subsequent passes log "no open stories remain" and the ticket is orphaned.
- Recovery today is fully manual: delete the work branch, delete `state.json`,
  and let sync re-add the ticket as `open`.

This is a robustness gap: a single interrupted pass (machine sleep, a
concurrent tree operation, a killed agent) permanently sidelines a ticket that
nothing is working on.

## Constraint / why it's subtle

The fix must distinguish a **stale** `in_progress` (no run actually working it)
from an **actively running** one, or it will yank a ticket out from under a live
run. Candidate signals: a process/lock check, a heartbeat/last-touched timestamp
on `state.json`, or an age threshold. This is adjacent to S-017 (mid-ticket
checkpoint/resume) but narrower: S-017 resumes from the last stage; this ticket
only needs to make a stranded `in_progress` eligible again (even if it restarts
from plan).

## Fix direction (implementer's choice)

- A reaper at the start of a poll that flips a *stale* `in_progress` (older than
  a configurable threshold and with no live lock holder) back to `open`; or
- Make `pick_next_story` treat a stale `in_progress` as eligible, gated by the
  same staleness signal.

Reuse the single-flight poll lock (`adw/locks.py`) as the liveness signal if it
fits — a held lock means a run is live; an `in_progress` with no held lock and a
stale `state.json` is a candidate for recovery.
acceptance_criteria: A ticket left `in_progress` by an interrupted run (no live run/lock) becomes
An `in_progress` ticket that **is** actively being worked (live lock / fresh
The staleness threshold is configurable (e.g. via `configs/budgets.json`).
Unit tests cover: a stale `in_progress` is reclaimed to eligible; a fresh /
Full suite stays green (`uv run pytest -q`).

stories.title: Sync's uncommitted prd.json write blocks checkout to a pre-existing work branch
stories.description:
## Problem

A manual cross-repo poll (`ADW_REPO=design-system python workflows/poll_once.py`)
crashed with:

```
subprocess.CalledProcessError: Command '['git', 'checkout', 'adw/GH-1']'
returned non-zero exit status 1.
```

The underlying git error is:

```
error: Your local changes to the following files would be overwritten by checkout:
        prd.json
Please commit your changes or stash them before you switch branches.
Aborting
```

## Root cause

`poll_once` runs `sync_fn` first, which rewrites the target's `prd.json` (GitHub
issue intake) and **leaves it uncommitted on `main`**. The backlog runner then
picks a story and calls `_ensure_work_branch("adw/<id>")`
(`adw/workflow_runner.py:79`). When the `adw/<id>` branch **already exists** from
a prior run, `_ensure_work_branch` takes the `git checkout <branch>` path
(line 81). Because the pre-existing branch has a divergent `prd.json`, git
refuses to switch over the uncommitted sync write and exits non-zero, aborting
the whole pass.

This is masked in the common case because work branches are usually created
fresh via `checkout -b` (the dirty tree carries over harmlessly). It only bites
when an `adw/<id>` branch is left behind by an earlier interrupted/failed run —
exactly the resume path we care about.

## Fix direction

Make the sync→checkout hand-off safe regardless of whether the work branch
already exists. Options (implementer's choice):

- Commit the sync's `prd.json` write (a bookkeeping commit, like
  `_commit_bookkeeping`) on `main` before `_ensure_work_branch`, so the tree is
  clean at the switch; or
- Stash-and-restore the uncommitted change around the checkout in
  `_ensure_work_branch`.

Keep it surgical and preserve the fresh-branch path's current behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A poll whose sync step modifies `prd.json` can pick a story whose `adw/<id>`
The fresh-branch path (no pre-existing `adw/<id>`) behaves exactly as today.
No sync write is silently lost: the synced `prd.json` content is preserved
A unit test reproduces the failure: a dirty `prd.json` plus a pre-existing
Full suite stays green (`uv run pytest -q`).
